Mirror Tower then mentioned that there is a person named Gu Chun Chuan, who passed the imperial examination in the year Wu Zi, and has already become a magistrate in the underworld, having been recommended by him. In this way, it is clear that in the underworld, things are just as they are in the human world.

    391

    ●卷八年月日时干支俱同者，六十年中，惟甲戌岁有甲戌月甲戌日甲戌时，乙酉岁有乙酉月乙酉日乙酉时，丙申岁有丙申月丙申日丙申时，丁未岁有丁未月丁末日丁未时，戊午岁有戊午月戊午日戊午时，己巳岁有己巳月己巳日己巳时，庚辰岁有庚辰月庚辰日庚辰时，辛卯岁有辛卯月辛卯日辛卯时，壬寅岁有壬寅月壬寅日壬寅时，癸亥岁有癸亥月癸亥日癸亥时。然甲戌之岁必有甲戌月，甲戌之日必有甲戌时，而甲戌之月不能必有甲戌日，其余皆然：故遇此难也。光绪六年三月十三日日加辰，是为庚辰岁庚辰月庚辰日庚辰时。杭有杨翁者，精于丛辰之学，死后营葬，有术者为择得此年此月此日此时，取四庚辰也。他术者以为不可用，议改用初六日癸酉。其家以翁素精此术，乃就其灵前拈阄决之，竟拈得四庚辰者，遂用以葬。友人丁松生与执绋焉，还过俞楼，为余言此，因为记之。

    Explanation

    391

    In a cycle of sixty years, there is only one instance where the year, month, day, and hour all share the same Heavenly Stem and Earthly Branch. Specifically, in the year Jia Xu (甲戌), the month, day, and hour are also Jia Xu; in the year Yi You (乙酉), the month, day, and hour are also Yi You; in the year Bing Shen (丙申), the month, day, and hour are also Bing Shen; in the year Ding Wei (丁未), the month, day, and hour are also Ding Wei; in the year Wu Wu (戊午), the month, day, and hour are also Wu Wu; in the year Ji Si (己巳), the month, day, and hour are also Ji Si; in the year Geng Chen (庚辰), the month, day, and hour are also Geng Chen; in the year Xin Mao (辛卯), the month, day, and hour are also Xin Mao; in the year Ren Yin (壬寅), the month, day, and hour are also Ren Yin; and in the year Gui Hai (癸亥), the month, day, and hour are also Gui Hai. However, while the year Jia Xu must have a Jia Xu month and a Jia Xu day, the month Jia Xu does not necessarily have a Jia Xu day, and similarly for the others. Therefore, encountering this phenomenon is difficult. On the 13th day of the third month of the sixth year of the Guangxu era, the hour of Chen, it was the year, month, day, and hour of Geng Chen. In Hangzhou, there was an old man named Yang who was skilled in the study of "concentrated celestial phenomena." After his death, when his burial was being arranged, an astrologer selected this year, month, day, and hour for the burial, taking the four Geng Chen. Other astrologers believed it was not suitable and suggested using the 6th day of the month, Gui You. The family, knowing that the old man was particularly skilled in this art, consulted his spirit before his tomb and drew lots to decide. They ended up drawing the four Geng Chen and proceeded with the burial. A friend named Ding Songsheng participated in the funeral procession and later passed by Yu Lou, where he recounted this story to me, and I recorded it.

    392

    南斗注生，北斗注死，古有此说，是冥冥之中固有簿书记载矣。世间每遇水火刀兵诸劫，辄有人梦见冥中缮写册籍，疑亦理之所有也。咸丰之初，粤寇萌芽，有海盐人查某者，梦至一处，见文书堆积如山，数十人缮写，犹若不及。楹间悬一联云：“弱柳琼箫仙有劫”，“落花铜鼓佛无灵”。意不可解，而其语则颇可诵也。

    Explanation

    392

    The Southern Dipper records births, and the Northern Dipper records deaths; this saying has existed since ancient times, indicating that there are indeed records in the unseen realm. In the world, whenever disasters of water, fire, weapons, and warfare occur, people often dream of writing ledgers in the underworld, which seems to be a natural phenomenon. At the beginning of the Xianfeng era, the Taiping Rebellion began to take root. There was a man from Haiyan named Zha, who dreamed of arriving at a place where documents were piled up like mountains, and dozens of people were writing, as if they could not keep up. On the pillars hung a couplet that read: "Even the delicate willow and jade flute are subject to fate," and "Even the falling flowers and bronze drums lack divine intervention." The meaning was elusive, but the language was very beautiful to recite.

    393

    黄冈县易氏女子化为男，余已载在第一卷矣。有佣媪宋姓者言：河南兰仪县乡间某氏女子，已许嫁矣。某翁无子，性好善，其女病数日，忽化为男。言于父母，验之信，乃使人告之夫家。其亲家翁曰：“此翁为善之报也。吾有弱女，愿即以嫁之。亲者无失为亲，不亦善乎？”某翁大喜，仍谐二姓之好。后生子女，而母故荏弱，凡衣履缝纫之事，父辄分任其劳，盖素习女红，虽为男子，犹未忘故技也。

    　　牙牌之戏，相传起于宋宣和时，其来久矣。近世有《牙牌数》一书，借以占卜，盖亦古者棋卜樗蒲卜之类也。兄子履卿言：光绪己卯岁江南乡试，无锡诸士子于榜前占《牙牌数》，其辞云：“大开围场，射鹿得獐。顾盼自喜，中必叠双。”是科无锡县中式者二人，一顾姓，一章姓。顾字明见数中，而“射鹿得獐”句暗影“章”字，尤为巧合也。

    Explanation

    393

    A woman from the Yi family in Huanggang County transformed into a man, and I have already recorded this in the first volume. A servant named Song, who works for a family in LanYi County, Henan Province, recounted that a woman from a certain family in the countryside had already been betrothed. The old man of that family had no son and was naturally inclined to do good. One day, his daughter fell ill for several days and suddenly transformed into a boy. She told her parents, who verified the truth of the transformation. They then sent a messenger to inform her future husband's family. The father-in-law of the family said, "This is a reward for this old man's good deeds. I have a weak daughter, and I wish to marry her to him. The relatives will not lose their kinship, which is also a good thing!" The old man was very pleased and continued the marriage between the two families. Later, they had children, but the mother remained frail. The father took on the tasks of making clothes and sewing, as he had previously practiced needlework. Even though he was now a man, he had not forgotten his old skills.

    The game of yapai (牙牌), a traditional Chinese card game, is said to have originated during the reign of Emperor Huizong of the Song Dynasty and has a long history. In recent times, there is a book called Yapai Shu (《牙牌数》) that uses the game for divination, similar to ancient methods of divination using chess pieces and dice. My nephew, Fuqing, mentioned that during the Jiangnan provincial examination in 1899, the year of Gengyin under the Guangxu era, several scholars from Wuxi consulted the Yapai Shu before the results were announced. The divination said, "A large hunting ground is opened, and a deer is shot but turns out to be a roe deer. Looking around with self-satisfaction, the result will definitely be a double win." That year, two scholars from Wuxi passed the examination: one was surnamed Gu, and the other was surnamed Zhang. The character "Gu" was clearly visible in the divination, while the phrase "射鹿得獐" (a deer is shot but turns out to be a roe deer) subtly hinted at the surname "Zhang," making the prediction particularly coincidental.

    394

    任司马，江苏人，谈者不言其名字。需次浙中，寓舍频有怪异。一日，失去洋钱三枚，正相诘问，忽空中语曰：“我所假也，三日当奉还。”越三日，果得其二。问尚有其一何在，曰：“此尚须迟一日耳。”越日，果得之。嗣后频有假贷，或偿或否，而不见其形。司马偶坐听事前，忽见一人过其檐下，其形夭矫，几及丈余，疑蛇精也。司马有女公子刺绣于楼，晨起视之，则于空处添绣一人面，异而传观。空中语曰：“此我所为，即我之像也。汝曹欲识我貌，观此可矣。”其面不辨为男为女，惟上有两角，面色白，角则黄色。其余口鼻之类，尚与人无大异。

    Explanation

    394

    A Military Governor from Jiangsu, his name and title were not mentioned by those who spoke of him. During his temporary assignment in central Zhejiang, his lodging frequently experienced strange occurrences. One day, he lost three foreign coins, and just as he was questioning what had happened, a voice suddenly came from the air, saying, "I borrowed them, and I will return them in three days." After three days, he indeed found two of the coins. He asked about the whereabouts of the third, and the voice replied, "This one will be returned in a day." The next day, he found it. Afterwards, there were frequent borrowings, some of which were repaid and others not, but the form of the borrower was never seen. One day, the Military Governor was sitting in front of his office when he suddenly saw a figure passing under his eaves, its form twisting and serpentine, almost ten feet long, and he suspected it was a snake spirit. His daughter, a noblewoman, was embroidering in the upstairs room. In the morning, she noticed that a face had been added to the blank space of her embroidery, which she found strange and showed to others. The voice from the air said, "This is what I have done, it is my image. You all wish to recognize my appearance, look here." The face was indistinguishable as male or female, with only two horns on top, the face being white, and the horns yellow. The rest, such as the mouth and nose, were still somewhat similar to humans.

    395

    至今此像尚在司马家。司马迁屋避之，辄随之至，不知其究竟如何也。咸丰间，江都符南樵孝廉主讲松江之柘林书院，与同人为扶箕之戏。有女仙降坛，自称唐时人，姓韩名碧霞，父诚为苏州刺史。时江苏尚陷于贼，问何时贼平。箕笔作一诗，有二句云：“扫开云雾见青天，正在辽东献豕年。”后贼平在癸亥岁，符豕年之语。又苏州陆凤石润庠之封翁九芝先生，于咸丰间扶箕，问凤石科名事。箕笔书七绝一首，其末句云：“金阶拜向卷帘时。”后凤石以同治甲戌大魁天下。是岁毅皇帝亲政，皇太后撤帘，箕诗“卷帘”之句，洵有先见。古称山鬼知一岁事，此殆不止一岁矣。

    Explanation

    395

    To this day, this image still remains in the Sima family. When Sima Qian tried to avoid it, it followed him wherever he went, and it is unknown what its ultimate fate is. During the Xianfeng era, Fu Nanqiao, a scholar-official from Jiangdu, was the headmaster of the Zhe Lin Academy in Songjiang, and he played the game of spirit writing with his fellow scholars. A female immortal descended to the altar, identifying herself as a person from the Tang Dynasty, surnamed Han and named Bixia, whose father was the governor of Suzhou. At that time, Jiangsu was still under the control of rebels, and they asked when the rebels would be defeated. The spirit writing produced a poem, which included two lines: "Clearing away the clouds to see the blue sky, it is the year when pigs are offered in Liaodong." Later, the rebels were defeated in the year 1863, which matched the phrase about the year of pigs. Additionally, during the Xianfeng era, Mr. Jiuzhi, the father-in-law of Lu Fengshi, played the game of spirit writing and asked about Lu Fengshi's success in the imperial examinations. The spirit writing produced a seven-character quatrain, with the final line reading: "Bowing on the golden steps when the curtain is drawn." Later, Lu Fengshi won the highest honor in the imperial examinations in the year 1874. That year, Emperor Tongzhi personally took charge, and the Empress Dowager withdrew her regency. The spirit writing's phrase "drawing the curtain" indeed had foresight. It is said that mountain spirits know the affairs of a year, and this seems to go beyond just one year.

    396

    昆山县有地名茜墩。咸丰四年夏，有村童金姓者，年十五岁，刺小舟放鸭湖中。正夤缘苇间，俄油然作云，狂飙怒吼，白浪如山。人与舟俱被风挟至空中，俯视云气迷，下不见地。自思设或坠下，必糜烂矣。忽来一老者，衣黄色衣，须发皤然，手执麈尾，指曰：“去，去！”人船俱从空而下，坠于平地，一无所伤，距其家三里矣。观者环堵，咸共叹异。牵其舟下水，送还其家。

    　　苏州开元寺前有大石狮二，岁月既久，颇著灵异。盘门以内，地甚荒僻，居人多以蔬圃为业。一夜三更后，守圃者见有物在圃中啮食蔬菜，其形庞然，不知何物。亟返呼众共视，则已杳矣。嗣后连夕皆然，乃纠众伺之。夜半，物果至，突出噪逐，物狂奔而去，至寺前乃止。迫视之，石狮也。遂铸大铁钉钉之，自此不复夜出。

    Explanation

    396

    In Kunshan County, there is a place named Xiadun. In the summer of the fourth year of the Xianfeng era, a village boy surnamed Jin, aged fifteen, was paddling a small boat to release ducks into the lake. As he was passing through the reeds, suddenly dark clouds gathered, and a fierce wind roared, with white waves as high as mountains. Both the boy and the boat were carried into the air by the wind. Looking down, the clouds were so thick that the ground was not visible. He thought to himself that if he were to fall, he would surely be crushed. Suddenly, an old man appeared, wearing a yellow robe, with white hair and beard, holding a fly whisk in his hand and pointing, saying, "Go, go!" Both the boy and the boat fell from the air and landed safely on the ground, unharmed, three miles from his home. People gathered around and all marveled at the strange event. They pulled the boat back into the water and returned it to his home.

    In front of the Kaiyuan Temple in Suzhou, there are two large stone lions. After many years, they became quite famous for their supernatural powers. Inside the Pan Gate, the area is very remote and desolate, and the residents mostly make a living from vegetable gardens. One night after three o'clock, the guard saw something eating vegetables in the garden. It had a large shape, but it was unknown what it was. He quickly returned and called everyone to see, but it had already disappeared. Subsequently, this happened every night. They then gathered everyone to watch for it. At midnight, the creature indeed appeared, and they suddenly rushed out to chase it. The creature ran wildly away and stopped in front of the temple. Upon closer inspection, it was the stone lion. They then cast large iron nails and nailed it, and since then, it no longer came out at night.

    397

    朱祥麟，苏州人，素佻达，好渔色。偶饮于友人家，而归时夜已深矣，路无行人。至护龙街，见少妇独行，睨之美而艳。尾其后，徐以言挑之。妇微笑，招以手，大喜随之。行至一处，妇推门引朱入。屋不甚大，而陈设颇华，遂同入房中。镜奁脂，无不精美，床上则绮帷罗幔，绣被锦衾，更极富丽。妇解衣裙置施上，仅留衤日服，皓质呈露，促朱先寝。朱心荡不能自持，方欲登床，忽火光灼烁，十数人拥人，手执灯笼，皆有苏州府城隍字。俄顷之间，所见人物一时都杳，其地则在范庄前石栏杆外近水处也。骇极痴立，适有武弁行夜至此，知为鬼迷，送之归。此等景象，必溺鬼幻为之，使非神灯一照，是人必于温柔乡中失足矣，可不惧哉！

    Explanation

    397

    Zhu Xianglin, a native of Suzhou, was naturally carefree and unrestrained, and loved to pursue beautiful women. One day, he drank at a friend's house and returned late at night when there were no people on the road. He reached Hulong Street and saw a young woman walking alone. He glanced at her beauty and found her attractive. He followed her from behind and gently teased her with words. The woman smiled and beckoned to him with her hand. Greatly pleased, he followed her. They arrived at a place where the woman pushed open the door and led Zhu inside. The house was not very large, but it had luxurious furnishings, and they entered the room together. The room was adorned with mirrors, makeup boxes, and cosmetics, all of which were exquisite. On the bed were luxurious curtains, embroidered quilts, and brocade bedding, even more splendid. The woman undressed and placed her clothes on a stand, leaving only a simple garment, revealing her pure and fair skin. She urged Zhu to sleep first. Zhu's heart was stirred, and he couldn't control himself, just as he was about to climb onto the bed. Suddenly, bright firelight appeared, and several people surrounded him, holding lanterns with the words of Suzhou City God. In a moment, all the people and objects disappeared, and the location was near a stone railing outside Fan Zhuang, close to the water. Terrified, he stood there in a daze. Just then, a military official passed by at night, knowing that Zhu was bewitched by ghosts, and took him back home. Such a scene must have been created by ghostly illusions. If not for the divine light illuminating, he would have stumbled in the land of pleasure. How can one not be afraid!

    398

    山东登莱青道署有朱藤二本，数百年物也。其藤蜿蜒萦绕，所荫可数亩许。花时紫艳纷披，掩映户牖间，清香袭人。相传其树有神，当风清月白，往往见之。民间有疾病者，得其一花一叶，即可疗治，以是争来祷祀，香火不绝。署中有空屋数间，花落辄扫置其中，以供病者之求。凡新官入署，必祭焉。潘伟如中丞尝为是官，循例致祭，并以酒一大瓮浇其根土。其夜两花神皆见形，一为老翁，朱颜皓首，长髯及腹；一为妇人。署中人皆见之。是年花开尤盛。

    Explanation

    398

    The Office of the Dengzhou, Laizhou, and Qingzhou Governors in Shandong Province had two wisteria plants, which had existed for several hundred years. The vines twisted and coiled, shading an area of several acres. When the flowers bloomed, they were purple and scattered in beauty, partially hiding the doors and windows, with a fragrant scent wafting over people. It was said that the tree had a spirit, and when the wind was clear and the moon was bright, it was often seen. Among the people who were sick, obtaining one flower or one leaf could treat their ailments, and because of this, people came to pray and worship, with the incense and candles never stopping. In the office, there were several empty rooms, and when the flowers fell, they were always swept and placed in the rooms to provide for the requests of the sick. All new officials entering the office must worship it. Zhongcheng Pan WeiRu, who once held this position, followed custom and offered sacrifices, and poured a large jar of wine over the roots. That night, both flower spirits appeared: one was an old man with a ruddy complexion and white hair, and a long beard down to the belly; the other was a woman. All the people in the office saw them. That year, the flowers bloomed especially profusely.

    399

    苏州人姜少甫与其友石某，至杭州天竺烧香，瞻礼既毕，循途而归。至万松岭下，石忽腹痛，即于道旁遗矢焉。忽有一鹰自空而下，攫其帽以去。急起夺之，而鹰愈翔愈远，高入云际，不可复见。二人大骇，踉跄归舟。至暮而石病作，头痛身热，颇不可堪。夜半，忽梦入一城。问城中人此为何地，或告之曰：“绍兴嵊县也。”俄至一庙，中有碑甚高，刻“申庄”二大字，而日间所失之帽俨然在碑上。大喜，引手欲取之。旁一僧止之曰：“不能，不能！且待四年后来取。”遂寤。次日病殊未减。姜与余门下士王梦薇有旧，以梦薇能医，令石力疾就诊，因自述其梦。梦薇诊其脉，但感受风寒耳，初无他也，授之以方，匆匆辞去，云：“即日还苏。”不知究竟如何。

    Explanation

    399

    A Suzhou man named Jiang Shaofu and his friend Shi went to Tianzhu Temple in Hangzhou to burn incense and pay their respects. After completing the ritual, they began their return journey. As they reached the foot of Wansong Hill, Shi suddenly felt abdominal pain and relieved himself on the roadside. Suddenly, a hawk swooped down from the sky and snatched his hat away. They quickly tried to snatch it back, but the hawk flew further and further away, high into the clouds, disappearing from sight. The two men were greatly frightened and staggered back to their boat. By evening, Shi's illness began, with a headache and fever that were quite unbearable. At midnight, he suddenly dreamed of entering a city. He asked the people in the city what place this was, and someone told him, "It is Shengxian County in Shaoxing." Soon, he arrived at a temple, where there was a very high stele inscribed with the two large characters "Shen Zhuang." To his great delight, his lost hat was clearly visible on the stele. He reached out to take it, but a monk beside him stopped him, saying, "Cannot, cannot! Wait four years to come and take it." Then he woke up. The next day, his illness did not decrease much. Jiang had an old acquaintance with Wang Mengwei, my disciple, who was skilled in medicine. Because of this, he ordered Shi to rush to see Wang, and then described his dream. Wang diagnosed his pulse, saying it was just a common cold, nothing else, and gave him a prescription. He hurriedly took his leave, saying, "I will return to Suzhou today." It was not known what would happen in the end.

    400

    余谓此人之病，自因风寒，当腹痛时已伏病根，鹰之攫帽，会逢其适。既有是事，因有是梦，梦由心造，与病初无涉也。苏州陆墓村人某甲，当兵乱时，于途间得人家所弃子，畜之为子。比长，为取妻。甲夫妇初不知子之父母为谁，其子并不知别有父母也。光绪六年，甲妇病笃，死而复苏，呼其子，语之曰：“我在冥中，见尔母乞还其子，我已许之矣。汝母某氏，汝父则尚在人间，姓某名某，住苏州城中某处。汝宜携尔妇归，无使我失信于尔母也。”言已遂卒。其子以为乱命，不之信。次日，有苏州人至，其姓名与甲妇所言同，索还其子。甲问：“事越二十年，何忽见索？”其人曰：“夜间亡妇见梦，言曩所失子在君家。今向君家妇乞还，承其见许，故来此，愿与子俱归也。”甲问其家住何所，则亦符合。乃谓其子曰：“此真尔父矣。吾幸已有子，汝携妇以归，可也。”其子始泣谢而去。

    Explanation

    400

    I was ill due to a cold, and when I had abdominal pain, the root of the illness was already present. The hawk seizing the hat happened to meet its suitability. Since there was this event, there was this dream, and dreams are created by the mind, which has nothing to do with the illness. A man named Mr. A from Lumu Village in Suzhou found an abandoned child on the road during a time of military turmoil and raised him as his own son. When he grew up, he married him a wife. Mr. A and his wife initially did not know who the child's parents were, and the child did not know that he had other parents. In the sixth year of the Guangxu era, Mr. A's wife fell gravely ill, died, and then revived. She called her son and told him, "I saw your mother begging to take back her child in the afterlife. I have already agreed to it. Your mother is a certain lady, your father is still alive, his surname is a certain one, his name is a certain one, and he lives in a certain place in Suzhou. You should take your wife back with you, so as not to make me break my promise to your mother." After saying this, she died. The son thought this was a disorderly command and did not believe it. The next day, a man from Suzhou arrived, and his name was the same as what Mr. A's wife had said, and he reclaimed his child. Mr. A asked, "The matter is more than twenty years old. Why are you suddenly reclaiming him?" The man said, "The deceased wife appeared in a dream at night and said that the child I lost in the past is in your home. I accepted the permission, so I came here, and I wish to take the child back with me." Mr. A asked where the man lived, and it also matched. Then he told his son, "This is truly your father. I am fortunate to already have a son, so you can take your wife back with you." The son then wept and thanked him before leaving.

    401

    杭州西湖玛瑙寺故址前，有大石一方，俯临湖ぞ，名停鹰石。有某寺僧拟刻《金刚经》于上，乃就石旁编木为棚，招集工匠于其中，将于次日奏刀焉。忽有一人衣黑色衣，青色半臂，至棚中与工匠闲话，问：“尔曹来此奚为？”告以故。其人曰：“俟刻石时，宜先告我。苟不我告，必贻后悔。”众工因询其所居，曰：“即在石旁不远耳。”及次日，将事刻，工匠遍觅其人不得。偶言于松木场茶寮中，有闻者曰：“石旁固无居人，得无龟精乎？”骇而问之，曰：“此石下旧有龟精，人往往有见之者，其大如五石瓠，不为人害。今刻经于其上，则彼为所镇压，不得出入自由，宜彼之意有所不欲也。西湖南北两山为石多矣，可刻之石亦必不少，奚必此？盍与僧再议之。”众疑信参半。是日因循未刻。越宿，而众工或病于目，或病于臂，乃觉其有异，言于僧，改刻他处。

    Explanation

    401

    At the former site of the Marbles Temple in West Lake, Hangzhou, there stands a large stone that overlooks the lake, known as the Eagle Stopping Stone. A monk from a certain temple intended to engrave the Diamond Sutra on this stone, so he built a wooden canopy beside the stone and gathered craftsmen within it, planning to begin work the next day. Suddenly, a man dressed in a black robe and wearing a blue half-sleeve arrived at the canopy and chatted with the craftsmen. He asked, "What brings you all here?" They explained their purpose. The man said, "When you start carving the stone, you should inform me first. If you do not inform me, you will surely regret it." The craftsmen then inquired about his residence, and he replied, "It is not far from the stone." On the next day, as they were about to begin the engraving, the craftsmen searched everywhere for the man but could not find him. Someone mentioned this in the tea pavilion of the Pine Wood Field, and a listener said, "There are no residents near the stone, could it be the turtle spirit?" They were startled and asked, "This stone has an old turtle spirit beneath it. People often see it, and it is as large as a five-stone gourd. It does not harm humans. Now, if you engrave the sutra on top of it, it will be suppressed and lose its freedom to move in and out. This is why it does not want it. There are many stones in the hills north and south of West Lake, and there must be many suitable ones for engraving. Why choose this one? Why not discuss it again with the monk?" The craftsmen were half-believing and half-doubting. That day, they did not proceed with the engraving. The next day, some of the craftsmen suffered from eye problems, while others had arm problems. They realized something was amiss and told the monk, who then decided to engrave the sutra elsewhere.

    402

    某甲，农人也，尝以争田致人于死，后即于所争之田获稻。忽雷雨骤至，甲呼其子偕归，其子尚幼，随之行。见空中人马喧阗，无虑数十人，或手秉炬火，或负一物若鱼网者。既近，有青衣者二人夺其父所获稻去。又数人以网蒙其父头，炬火即从之下击，辟历一声，其子惊仆，起视，则其父死矣。曩所见诸人，皆化为云气，腾空而去。

    Explanation

    402

    A certain man, a farmer, once caused someone's death by disputing over land. Later, he harvested rice on the very land that was the subject of the dispute. Suddenly, a thunderstorm came, and the farmer called out to his young son to return home with him. The boy followed his father. They saw a noisy crowd of people and horses in the air, about several dozen in number, some holding torches in their hands, others carrying an object like a fishing net. When they got closer, two people dressed in green took away the rice his father had harvested. Several more people then covered his father's head with a net, and the torch immediately struck down from below. A thunderclap, and his son fainted in shock. When he got up and looked, his father was dead. The people he had seen before all turned into clouds and soared into the sky and left.

    403

    桐乡周铁崖士钢，少补博士弟子员，屡试秋闱不售。一日，自佗处归，夜泊舟村落间。望见临水一家，有楼翼然，楼窗外有碧火如环，旋转不定。异而呼舟人讯之，舟人见而骇曰：“缢鬼求代，多作此状，吾屡见之矣。此家必有将缢死者，慎勿声，鬼为人所觉，且移祸于人。”周奋然曰：“见人死而不救，非夫也！”登岸，叩门大呼。其家出问，告以故，大惊，盖姑妇方勃溪，妇泣涕登楼，姑犹呶呶未已也。闻周言，亟共登楼，排闼而入，妇手持带立床前，神已痴矣。呼之逾时始觉，举家共劝慰之，乃已。周次日抵家，梦一老人谓之曰：“子勇于为善，宜食其报。”周曰：“此何足为善。必欲食报者，他不敢望，敢问我于科名何如？”老人笑而示以掌，掌中有“何可成”三字。寤而叹曰：“科名无望矣！”其明年，竟登贤书。是科主试者为何公，始悟梦语之巧合也。

    Explanation

    403

    A Scholar from Tongxiang Named Zhou Tiyan

    Zhou Tiyan, a young scholar from Tongxiang, served as a student in a Confucian academy but repeatedly failed the autumn imperial examinations. One day, returning from another place, he anchored his boat in a village area at night. He saw a house by the water with a building that looked like wings, and outside the window, there was green fire spinning unpredictably. Surprised, he called the boatman to ask about it. The boatman, seeing the phenomenon, was terrified and said, "This is often a sign of a ghost seeking a substitute, and I have seen it many times. This family must have someone who is about to hang themselves. Be careful not to make a sound, for if the ghost is discovered by people, it will transfer the misfortune to someone else." Zhou, moved by the situation, said, "To see someone die and not save them is not worthy of being a man!" He then went ashore, knocked on the door, and shouted loudly. The family came out to ask what was happening, and he explained the situation. They were greatly surprised, as it turned out that the mother-in-law and daughter-in-law were quarreling, and the daughter-in-law, crying, had gone to the top of the building. The mother-in-law was still arguing with her. Hearing Zhou's words, they quickly went up the stairs, pushed open the door, and entered. The woman was standing in front of the bed, holding a rope, and her mind was already in a daze. They called to her for a long time before she finally realized what was happening. The whole family comforted her together, and she calmed down. The next day, Zhou returned home and had a dream where an old man told him, "You are courageous in doing good, and you should receive your reward." Zhou replied, "How can this be considered good? If one must receive a reward, I do not dare to hope for anything else, but I dare to ask you about my career in the imperial examinations." The old man laughed and showed him his palm, which had the three characters "何可成" (How can it succeed). Upon waking, Zhou sighed and said, "There is no hope for my career in the imperial examinations!" The following year, he unexpectedly made the list of successful candidates. The chief examiner of that examination was Mr. He, and only then did Zhou realize the coincidence between his dream and reality.

    404

    同治乙丑年，浙江补行乡试。是年夏，嵊县有人梦至一庙，庙中有神南面坐。其旁胥吏甚夥，各负一牌，呈神坐前。神执笔于牌上点之，乃各负之出。梦者随而问曰：“此何牌也？”负牌者曰：“牌上姓名，皆今年乡试中式人也。”梦者见一牌上大书“裘治成”三字，其旁尚有小字数行。问此小字何也，曰：“皆其人所行阴德也。”寤而访之，则邑中诸生果有裘治成其人者，往告以梦。裘笑曰：“吾不应试十余年矣，精力衰颓，学问荒落，自分此生永谢场屋，恐不能践君幻梦也。”梦者遍告裘之姻族，咸纵臾之，乃赴试，竟举于乡。或问裘有何阴德，裘曰无之。固问，乃曰：“前年族人有一婢，将置之死，吾力救而免之，且为择婿而嫁焉。此或一事乎？余事则非所知矣。”盖无心之善，作者犹不自觉，而鬼神则已纪录之也。

    Explanation

    404

    In the year of Tongzhi, the second year of the ox, Zhejiang province held a supplementary provincial examination. That summer, a person from Sheng County dreamed of arriving at a temple, where there was a deity seated facing south. Around the deity were many clerks and officials, each carrying a tablet and presenting it before the deity. The deity wrote on the tablet, and then each carried it out. The dreamer followed and asked, "What is this tablet?" The person carrying the tablet replied, "The names on the tablet are all those who passed the provincial examination this year." The dreamer saw a tablet with the large characters "Qiu Zhicheng" written on it, and there were several lines of small print beside it. The dreamer asked what the small print meant, and the person replied, "These are all the good deeds done secretly by that person." Upon waking, the dreamer went to find out and discovered that indeed there was a person named Qiu Zhicheng among the scholars in the county. He went to tell him about the dream. Qiu smiled and said, "I have not taken the examination for over ten years; my energy and spirit have declined, and my learning has become neglected. I believe this life will never see me in the examination hall again, and I fear I cannot fulfill your dream." The dreamer then told all of Qiu's relatives and friends, and they all encouraged him. He then took the examination and was successful. Someone asked Qiu what good deeds he had done, and Qiu said he did not know. When pressed, he said, "Last year, a servant in my clan was about to be put to death, and I saved her and arranged for her marriage. This may be one good deed, but I do not know of any others." It is said that unintentional good deeds, even if the doer is unaware, are recorded by the gods.

    405

    会稽王湘舟济泰，工为制举文，而困于场屋。性好施予。一日，有里人以砚来，求易钱一千。视其砚，凡石也，却之。其人颜色惨沮，问其故，则其妇方产，势甚危殆，欲以此砚易钱招白洋媪婆。白洋媪婆者，越中乳医之最良者也。遂留其砚而予之钱。久之，念此妇未知已产未，使问之其家，则妇死矣，无以敛，其姑亦老且病，号兆欲自尽。王恻然，急取敝衣数袭，洋钱十枚，亲送与之。至咸丰乙卯岁，元旦之夕，梦一妇人向之叩首，曰：“妾即往岁以产而亡者也。”手以一黄纸条示之，有朱书曰“臣十七”。寤而不解所谓。是岁应秋试，入闱中，所坐号适为臣字十七。及题目出，文思泉涌，榜发中式。

    Explanation

    405

    The Prince of Kuaiji, Xiangzhou Jitai, was skilled in composing essays for the imperial examinations but struggled to pass them. He had a generous nature and enjoyed giving to others. One day, a villager brought a砚 to him, asking to exchange it for one thousand coins. Upon seeing the砚, he realized it was just a common stone and rejected it. The villager's face turned sad and dejected, and when asked why, he explained that his wife had just given birth, and the situation was very dangerous. He wanted to hire a midwife from Baiyang with the砚. The prince then kept the砚 and gave him the money. Later, he thought about whether the woman had given birth or not, so he sent someone to ask her family. It turned out that the woman had died, and her mother-in-law, who was old and sick, was wailing and wanting to commit suicide. The prince felt compassion and quickly took several sets of old clothes and ten pieces of foreign currency, personally delivering them to her. That year, he took the autumn examination and entered the examination hall. His seat number happened to be seventeen. When the questions were announced, ideas flowed like a spring, and he passed the examination. One night during the New Year of the Xianfeng era, he dreamed of a woman bowing to him, saying, "I am the woman who died in childbirth last year." She handed him a yellow paper strip with the words "臣十七" written in red. He woke up without understanding its meaning.

    406

    山阴倪某，幼时曾一赴童子试，不售，辄弃去。善治生，家固殷，赈人极长厚，乡里称善人也。同治癸酉岁，年五十余矣。其邻有张君者，故粤东一县丞，以病免归，自言判冥中事，倪亦未之信。是岁之春，劝倪赴乡试，倪笑谢之，张言之不已。一日，密谓之曰：“君忘十五年前事邪？完人名节，上帝所重，君得科名，职此之故，其必往，毋自误也。”倪忆十五年前有亲串家一少妇，夫亡守节，其兄公以莫须有之事污蔑之，谋出之而夺其财。事垂成矣，为力白之乃已。此事张所不知也，始心动。然久抛举业，惧不中程式，乃先作文十余篇，并乞名手点定。及入场，《四书》题三道皆在其中，录之而出，榜发中式。乃叹曰：“张君果判冥事，不吾欺也。”

    Explanation

    406

    A man from Shan Yin named Ni, when young, once took the preliminary examination for scholars but did not pass and thus abandoned it. He was skilled in managing his livelihood, his family was originally wealthy, and he was extremely generous and kind in helping others, earning the reputation of a good person in his village. In the year 1873 during the Tongzhi era, he was more than fifty years old. Among his neighbors was a gentleman named Zhang, who was formerly a county magistrate in Guangdong and retired due to illness. Zhang claimed to judge matters in the afterlife, but Ni did not believe him. That spring, Zhang urged Ni to take the provincial examination. Ni laughed and declined, but Zhang kept talking about it. One day, Zhang privately said to him, "Have you forgotten the matter from fifteen years ago? Preserving one's reputation and integrity is what Heaven values. You will gain academic success for this reason. You must go, do not mislead yourself." Ni recalled that fifteen years ago, a young woman from a relative's family had her husband die and remained chaste. Her brother falsely accused her of something unfounded, planned to have her expelled and seize her property. The matter was almost completed when I worked hard to clear her name. Zhang did not know about this matter, but he began to be moved. However, he had long abandoned his studies and feared he would not meet the standards. So he first wrote more than ten essays and asked a renowned scholar to review them. When he entered the examination hall, three of the four Confucian classics questions were among them. He recorded them and left. The results were announced, and he passed. He then sighed, "Zhang indeed judges matters in the afterlife; he did not deceive me."

    407

    乐平汪宗伯守和，幼时读书家塾，私与侪辈斗蟋蟀，角胜负。宗伯公所蓄者巨喙而黑质，每斗必胜。公宝爱之，夜闭之笼中，而置诸庭，使吸清露。及旦取视，则死矣，两股断焉。懊恨殊甚，迹其所在，得一股于石罅，群蚁曳之，将入于穴，始知为蚁所杀也。公怒，思有以报之。乃至市廛买爆竹，剥取其火药，以膻物引蚁出穴，布火药于地而火之。群蚁数千，同时而毙，为之抚掌称快。既而悔焉，曰：“嘻！亦太甚矣！”遂不复畜蟋蟀。及嘉庆庚午岁，公以少詹事授奉天府丞兼学政，与盛京将军萨公善。一日，有布衣客诣将军辕门求见，萨公见之。

    Explanation

    407

    Wang Zongbo, a high-ranking official from Leping, used to read at home when he was young. He would secretly play cricket fighting with his peers, competing for victory. The cricket that Lord Zongbo kept had a large beak and a black body, and it always won in fights. Lord Zongbo treasured it greatly, closing it in a cage at night and placing it in the courtyard to drink the clear dew. When morning came and he checked on it, he found it dead, with its two legs broken. He was deeply regretful and angry, tracing its location, and found one leg in a crevice of a stone. A group of ants were dragging it, about to enter their nest. He then realized that the cricket had been killed by the ants. Lord Zongbo was furious and thought of a way to seek revenge. He went to the market to buy firecrackers, peeled off the gunpowder, and used stinky objects to lure the ants out of their nest. He spread the gunpowder on the ground and ignited it. Thousands of ants died simultaneously, and he clapped his hands in joy. Later, he regretted it, saying, "Ah! That was too extreme!" He no longer kept crickets after that. During the year Gengwu of the Jiaqing era, Lord Zongbo was appointed as Vice President of the Imperial Academy and concurrently as Governor of the Capital and Director of Education. He was friendly with General Sa of Shengjing. One day, a commoner visited the general's gate to request an audience, and General Sa received him.

    408

    长揖就坐，古貌俨然，自言：“以生人为冥吏，数至冥中决狱，今为汪学使一案而来。此案悬四十余年，仇家屡讼不休，今宜了结。以汪固端人，且其先世积累颇厚，自当右之。知公与汪善。明日请往省视，如汪有疾，属其家人勿相惊扰，亦勿投药饵，但谨守之，三日自愈也。”萨公异其言。明日，亲诣汪所，则汪得暴疾死，一署惶然。萨公如客言，使静守以待其苏。公果病三日而愈，自言得病之时，昏然不知人事，觉有人导之入一公廨。堂上有长官，服古衣冠，南面坐，面黧黑无髭，左右侍者甚众。长官问公曰：“有多人讼尔，知之否？”公告不知。长官命吏检簿籍示之，备载幼年杀蚁事。公谢曰：“事诚有之，今悔无及。

    Explanation

    408

    The guest bowed deeply and sat down, his appearance dignified and old-fashioned. He introduced himself, saying, "I am appointed as a ghost official while still alive, often going to the underworld to judge cases. I have come today to handle a case involving Mr. Wang, the Xueshi. This case has been unresolved for over forty years, with enemies repeatedly filing lawsuits without resolution. It is time to settle it. Mr. Wang is indeed a righteous person, and his ancestors have accumulated significant merit. Therefore, I should favor him. I know that you are good friends with Mr. Wang. Tomorrow, please visit him. If he falls ill, instruct his family not to disturb him, nor to give him medicine. Just take care of him carefully, and he will recover on his own in three days." Sa Gong was surprised by his words. The next day, he personally went to see Mr. Wang. However, Mr. Wang had suddenly fallen ill and died, causing great alarm in the office. Following the guest's advice, Sa Gong had his family keep a close watch on Mr. Wang, waiting for him to recover. Indeed, Mr. Wang fell ill for three days and then recovered. He recounted that when he fell ill, he was in a daze and unaware of what was happening. He felt someone guiding him into a public office. In the hall, there was a long official sitting facing south, wearing ancient clothing and no beard, with many attendants on either side. The long official asked Mr. Wang, "Do you know that many people are suing you?" Mr. Wang replied that he did not know. The long official then ordered the clerk to check the records and showed them to Mr. Wang, which detailed his childhood act of killing ants. Mr. Wang apologized, saying, "It is true that I did that, but I regret it now and cannot make amends."

    409

    ”长官命传诸讼者，则焦头烂额者无虑数千人，环呼索命。长官婉言谕之曰：“蚁与蟋蟀，皆虫也，尔等不合嘬食其所畜蟋蟀。童子无知，苟图泄愤，罔识轻重，遂成此狱。今既已降其科名，减其禄算，使其一生常有美中不足之处，亦足以示罚矣。”众哗然曰：“我辈止伤蟋蟀一命，彼戕我族类万余，岂此薄罚所能了邪！”言已大噪，其势汹汹，殆将用武。长官大怒曰：“汪某已膺冥罚，足以蔽辜。尔等幺麽微物，贪慕腥膻，污生人之厨灶，侵死者之墓隧，罪不胜诛。若再喧哗，当科罪如律！”众始惧而退。长官谓公曰：“此案已结，宜益为善，以赎前愆。”公乃苏。明日，即往谢将军。甫入，而前日之客出，遇于门，不交一言。

    Explanation

    409

    The magistrate ordered the summoning of all litigants, and there were countless victims with burnt heads and melted faces, who cried out in a circle for vengeance. The magistrate gently advised them, saying, "Ants and crickets are both insects. You should not have bitten and eaten the crickets they kept. The child was unaware and only sought to vent his anger, failing to recognize the gravity of the situation, and thus this case arose. Now that his rank and title have been reduced and his lifespan and fortune have been decreased, he will always have imperfections in his perfection, which is also sufficient to show the punishment." The crowd shouted, "We only injured one cricket, but he has killed tens of thousands of our kind. How can this light punishment suffice?" They spoke loudly, their momentum growing fierce, nearly ready to resort to violence. The magistrate became greatly angry and said, "Wang has already received divine punishment, which is sufficient to cover his guilt. You tiny and insignificant creatures, greedily craving for animal flesh, defile the kitchens of the living and intrude into the tombs of the dead. Your crimes are too numerous to be fully punished. If you continue to make a loud noise, you will be punished according to the law!" The crowd began to fear and retreated. The magistrate said to the official, "This case is now settled. You should do more good deeds to atone for your past sins." The official then awoke. The next day, he went to thank the general. As soon as he entered, the previous day's guest emerged and met him at the door, without exchanging a word.

    410

    萨公迎慰之曰：“昨日对簿甚劳。”公惊问：“何以知之？”以客言告，公亦缕述前事，且曰：“顷所遇之客，即冥中决狱者也，对之犹懔懔。若非祖德所庇，恐无生还之望矣。”乃悟客非常人也。公以乾隆壬子年举于乡，闱中初拟中第二名，后抑置十八名。及嘉庆丙辰岁廷对后，读卷者以前十本进呈，公裒然居首，仁庙更定为一甲第二名。其后官至尚书，以资格将晋协揆，竟不果。道光十六年薨于位，宣庙悼惜，赠太子太保，赐祭葬。故事，尚书殁于官，得予谥，礼臣以请。会畿辅旱，宵旰忧勤，此奏久留中，卒不得谥。殆即所谓降科名，减禄算，美中不足者欤？公以一代名臣，以戕群蚁，致罹冥谴，物命至重，可不慎欤！

    Explanation

    410

    Sa Gong greeted and comforted him, saying, "You must have been very tired from appearing in court yesterday." The official was surprised and asked, "How did you know?" Sa Gong told him what the guest had said, and the official also recounted the events in detail, adding, "The guest I met recently was the judge in the afterlife, and I still feel a sense of awe and fear when I think about it. If it weren't for the protection of my ancestors' virtue, I doubt I would have had any hope of returning to life." He then realized that the guest was no ordinary person. The official passed the provincial examination in the third year of Qianlong's reign (1772) and was initially ranked second in the examination, but was later demoted to eighteenth place. In the third year of Jiaqing's reign (1816), after the imperial examination, the examiners submitted the top ten essays, and the official stood out as the first. The emperor, known as the Benevolent Temple, reclassified him as the second place in the first class. Later, he rose to the position of Minister of Works, and with his qualifications, he was on the verge of becoming a high-ranking official, but ultimately did not succeed. In the sixteenth year of Daoguang's reign (1836), he died in office, and the emperor, known as the Temple of the Emperor, mourned and lamented his loss, posthumously granting him the title of Grand Tutor to the Crown Prince and bestowing a funeral sacrifice and burial. According to custom, when a minister dies in office, he is eligible for a posthumous title, and the minister of rites requested it. However, when the capital region suffered from drought, the emperor worked tirelessly day and night, and this request was left pending for a long time, ultimately not receiving a posthumous title. Perhaps this is what is meant by being demoted in rank, reduced in salary, and having some imperfections in perfection? As a renowned statesman of his generation, he killed a swarm of ants, and thus suffered punishment in the afterlife. The value of life is extremely important; how can one be careless?

    411

    道光间，有某观察者，性廉静，公事之暇，辄至斋中读书。一童子侍，丙夜始就内寝，日以为常。会届中秋节，制府某公素贪黩，馈献不满意，辄中伤之。观察不得已，枉法得白金五百两置书箧中，将以备书函并字之需。时秋高夜静，月色皎洁，方执卷咿唔，忽飘风入户，为之栗然。突见一女子戴黑纱幞头，衣粉红绡衣，近案而立。正惊顾间，其后又立一丈夫，赤发深目，须如猬毛，直前谓观察曰：“公所得金盍以为长者寿？”观察未之应，而女子袖中出白丸摩弄之，光灼烁如一匹练。观察惧，发箧出金陈案上，女子尽纳之袖。丈夫又谓观察曰：“以公平时固廉吏，故不相害。然此不义物，非公所宜有，谨将去，成公之廉。”言已，与女子俱出，倏忽不见。殆古剑侠之流欤？观察秘其事，旋移疾归。既归里，始与所亲者言之。

    Explanation

    411

    During the Daoguang era, there was a certain observer, a local official, who was known for his clean and quiet nature. In his spare time from official duties, he would go to his study to read. A young servant attended to him, and he would go to bed around midnight, making this his daily routine. Coinciding with the Mid-Autumn Festival, a governor-general, known for his usual corruption, would slander him if the gifts or offerings he received were unsatisfactory. The official had no choice but to pervert the law to obtain five hundred taels of silver, which he placed in his book chest to prepare for letters and writing materials. It was a high autumn night, with the moon shining brightly, and he was just about to read aloud when suddenly a gust of wind entered through the door, causing him to shiver with fear. Suddenly, he saw a woman wearing a black silk headscarf and a pink silk robe standing near the desk. While he was still startled and looking around, another man appeared behind her, with red hair, deep-set eyes, and a beard like a hedgehog's quills. He approached the official and said, "Why not use the gold you have obtained as a gift for the elderly?" The official did not respond. The woman then took out a white ball from her sleeve, played with it, and it shone brightly like a silken ribbon. The official was afraid, opened the chest, took out the gold, and placed it on the desk. The woman put all of it in her sleeve. The man then said, "Since you have always been a clean official, we have not harmed you. But this unjust gain is not something you should have. I will take it away to maintain your integrity." After saying this, he and the woman both left, disappearing suddenly. Probably an ancient swordswoman or swordsman? The official kept the matter secret and soon pretended to be ill and returned home. After returning home, he then told those close to him about it.

    412

    江西南昌府照墙后有老屋数间，故炮局也。咸丰三年，粤贼围城，营于沙井。其地有文孝庙，为贼所踞，庙墙坚厚，城中发炮击之，不能破。有人夜过照墙后，见黑面人数十辈从炮局出，言愿助官军杀贼。次日访之，则无人焉，知为炮神。言于官，掘地得大小炮甚多，重三千斤者十三尊，重四千斤者一尊。舁至章江门炮台，向文孝庙击之，墙壁皆毁，毙贼无算，遂解南昌之围。南昌人谓之神炮。长江水师提督黄公翼升，迎养其太夫人于金陵。

    Explanation

    412

    Behind the wall of the Nanchang Prefecture in Jiangxi Province, there stood several old houses, formerly a gunpowder depot. In the third year of the Xianfeng era (1853), the Taiping rebels besieged the city, setting up camp near Sha Jing. There was a Wen Xiang Temple in the area, which was occupied by the rebels. The temple's walls were thick and strong, and despite the city's efforts to bombard it, they could not be breached. One night, a person passing by the wall behind the old houses saw dozens of black-faced individuals emerge from the gunpowder depot, declaring their willingness to assist the government troops in fighting the rebels. The next day, when the location was investigated, no one was found, leading to the belief that these were divine beings. The authorities were informed, and upon digging up the ground, many cannons of various sizes were discovered, including thirteen weighing three thousand catties each and one weighing four thousand catties. These cannons were carried to the Zhanjiang Gate artillery platform and used to bombard the Wen Xiang Temple, destroying its walls and causing heavy casualties among the rebels, thus lifting the siege of Nanchang. The people of Nanchang called these cannons "divine cannons." Huang Yisheng, the commander of the Yangtze River Navy, brought and supported his mother-in-law in Nanjing.

    413

    太夫人偶得疾，梦神人告曰：“我因寇乱，身陷狮子街井中。如能救拔，必有以报，疾不足忧也。”太夫人觉，言所梦。公命人浚井，果得一石像，询之土人，曰：“从前江宁城中本有石将军庙，毁于贼，失其像。今所得者，即是也。”公因为立庙井旁，颇著灵异，至今香火不绝。惟石将军不知何人，相传为东晋人司马流。按《晋书。苏峻传》，峻遣将韩晃、张健等袭姑孰，进逼慈湖，杀于湖令陶馥及振威将军司马流，其他无所表见。千百年后尚能庙食人间，亦可异也。

    Explanation

    413

    The lady-in-waiting accidentally fell ill and dreamed that a divine being told her, "I was trapped in the well on Lion Street due to the banditry. If you can rescue me, I will surely repay you; do not worry about your illness." The lady-in-waiting awoke and recounted her dream. The gentleman ordered someone to dredge the well, and indeed, they found a stone statue. He asked the local people, who replied, "There used to be a Stone General Temple in Nanjing city, which was destroyed by bandits, and the statue was lost. What we found now is exactly that." The gentleman then built a temple by the well, which became quite famous for its supernatural powers. Until today, the incense continues to burn without interruption.

    As for the identity of the Stone General, it is unknown, but it is said to be a person from the Eastern Jin dynasty named Sima Liu. According to the History of the Jin Dynasty: Biography of Su Jun, Su Jun sent his generals Han Guang and Zhang Jian to attack Gusu, advancing towards Cihu, where they killed the magistrate of Yuhu, Tao Fu, and the General of Pacification, Sima Liu. He did not show any other significant achievements. Even after thousands of years, he can still be worshipped by people, which is indeed strange.

    414

    同治八年四月，江宁城糯米巷民宅中一井，晨汲得油数瓶，油尽始得水，明旦复汲亦然。如是数日，城内外悉闻知，观者填门。其家厌之，白县令封闭其井。长洲朱君孔彰时寓安品街，距其地不数十步，使人汲井验之，果如菜子油，入灯盏可然。

    　　溧阳崔，贫甚无以为家，寄居僧寺。读书至丙夜，忽闻寺后山上有车马声，启后户视之，无所见。俄而声益近，有人叩户曰：“小吴王至。”崔延之入，知其非人也。不敢发问。客曰：“君毋怖。我广西人，亦读书，籍诸生，为贼劫至江南，后入官军中，从张将军战死于此。闻君读书，窃所爱慕，山中无佳侣，冀聆雅音，聊慰幽寂。”又曰：“上帝命我主此山，封我为小吴王，我亦不复更念人间世。惟有一女年十五矣，小名阿鸾，今流落在广东香港。

    Explanation

    414

    In the fourth month of the eighth year of the Tongzhi era, a well in a civilian residence on Rice Noodle Alley in Nanjing produced several bottles of oil when water was drawn in the morning. The oil would run out before water appeared, and the next morning, drawing water again produced the same result. This continued for several days, and soon the entire city, both inside and out, heard about it. The crowd of spectators filled the doorways. The family grew tired of the situation and reported it to the county magistrate, who ordered the well to be sealed. Zhu Kongzhang from Changzhou, who was staying on Anpin Street, which was not far from the location, had someone draw water from the well to verify the claim. It was indeed like sesame oil and could be used to light lamps.

    Cui Zheng from Liyang was extremely poor, with nothing to make a home, and lived in a Buddhist temple. One night, while reading until the third watch, he suddenly heard the sound of carriages and horses on the mountain behind the temple. He opened the back door to look but saw nothing. Soon the sound became closer, and someone knocked on the door and said, "The Small Wu King has arrived." Cui Zheng entered and realized it was not a human. He did not dare to ask questions. The visitor said, "Do not be afraid. I am from Guangxi and also read books, registered as a scholar. I was captured by bandits and brought to Jiangnan, later joining the official army and fighting under General Zhang here, where I died. Hearing that you read, I secretly admire you. There are no good companions in the mountains. I hope to hear your refined voice to slightly comfort my loneliness." The visitor continued, "The Heavenly Emperor has appointed me to rule this mountain and bestowed upon me the title of Small Wu King. I no longer think about the human world. I have only one daughter who is fifteen years old, her small name is Aluan, and she is now stranded in Guangdong and Hong Kong."

    415

    君可至彼访之，如不嫌鄙陋，即以备箕帚可也。”言已别去。崔怪之，欲从其言往访女，然以道远，资粮扉屦，猝不易办，故未果也。居无何，小吴王又至，语崔曰：“君其无意乎？”崔以情告。曰：“君患无资，何不早告？此山之南有窖金，可发也。”即指示其处。崔发之，得白金五千两，乃如广东，访阿鸾于香港，果遇之青楼中。年未破瓜，犹处子也。风姿娟秀，粗通文翰，惟自幼流离，不自知其家世。崔以三千金赎之归，遂为夫妇。此事余闻之朱君孔彰，朱闻之于曾蓉舫。曾亦溧阳人，与崔相识也。

    Explanation

    415

    You may go there to visit her, and if you do not find it humble or unworthy, then you may consider her as your wife. After saying this, he left. Cui was surprised by this and wanted to follow his advice to visit the girl, but because the journey was far and the preparation of funds, food, and travel gear was not easily arranged, he did not succeed. After some time, the Little Wu King returned, and spoke to Cui, saying, "Are you not interested?" Cui told him the situation. He said, "You have a problem with funds, why did you not tell me earlier? There is a buried treasure of gold to the south of this mountain, you can dig it up." He then pointed out the location. Cui dug it up and obtained five thousand taels of silver, and then went to Guangdong to visit Aruan in Hong Kong, where he indeed met her in a brothel. She was still a virgin, not yet sixteen, and was still a maiden. She was graceful and beautiful, barely literate, but had been wandering since childhood and did not know her own family background. Cui redeemed her for three thousand taels of silver and brought her back home, and they became husband and wife. This story I heard from Zhu Jun Kongzhang, who in turn heard it from Zeng Rongfang. Zeng is also from Lishui and knew Cui.

    416

    黟县北郊有王某者，以工为讼牒，遂致殷富，营造新宅，颇极华美。一日，有数客造其邻比，问王某所居，邻人指新宅告之，遂入，然莫见其出也。越数日，新宅中忽火起，众奔救乃熄。自是火发无时，旋起旋灭，家中什物每提掷空中，悉皆破坏。煮饭于釜，饭熟，辄杂以泥沙，甚至粪函溺器，狼籍衾枕间。月余，不胜其扰，王某自作牒将讼诸城隍神，若有人厉声曰：“汝伎俩能欺人间官府耳，能欺明神欤！”祟益甚。王大惧，伏地自投，且言知罪，愿许悔过。又厉声曰：“速迁去，免尔死！”遂举家远避，空闭其宅。

    Explanation

    416

    In the northern suburbs of Yi County, there lived a man named Wang, who was skilled in drafting legal documents and thus became wealthy. He built a new house, which was quite luxurious and beautiful. One day, several guests visited his neighbors and asked about Wang's residence. The neighbors pointed to the new house and showed them the way. The guests entered, but no one saw Wang leave. A few days later, a fire suddenly broke out in the new house, and the crowd rushed to put it out. From then on, the fire flared up and died down at unpredictable times, and household items were thrown into the air and all were destroyed. When cooking rice in a pot, the rice would be mixed with mud and sand, and even the latrines and chamber pots were scattered among the bedding. After a month, Wang could no longer bear the disturbance. He wrote a petition to sue the City God. Suddenly, a loud voice said, "Your tricks can deceive human officials, but can they deceive a wise god?" The haunting became more severe. Wang was greatly frightened, fell to the ground, and confessed his sins, expressing his willingness to repent. The voice then shouted, "Leave quickly, or you will die!" Thus, Wang's entire family fled far away, leaving the house empty and closed.

    417

    沈岳艮，宜兴人，有膂力，而居心险恶。当粤贼之据常州也，受贼伪职，从之抗拒王师。每掠得妇女，必裸而淫之。禅国山东南有石洞，极深邃，妇女避乱者数百人入焉。沈积薪焚其洞，皆毙之。又杀周王庙僧数十人，而投神像于厕中。贼平逃出，竟无恙。一日，忽病狂，家人扶置床上，旋跃起以手相搏曰：“众妇人来索命！”又作妇人声曰：“彼有力，吾曹不能胜，宜招和尚来。”又大呼曰：“和尚来矣！”自是日夜呼[B17K]，不绝声。数日，家人守者稍倦，竟奔出，不知所之。半月后，有人言周王庙厕中溺死一人，家人往视，果沈也。遍身青黑，七窍流血，臭秽不可向迩。呜呼！其受报亦酷矣。

    Explanation

    417

    Shen Yuegen, a man from Yixing, possessed great physical strength but had a wicked heart. During the time when the Taiping rebels occupied Changzhou, he accepted a false position from the rebels and joined them in resisting the imperial forces. Whenever he captured women, he would strip them naked and rape them. In the southeastern part of Shandong, there was a deep and profound cave where hundreds of women seeking refuge from the chaos entered. Shen piled up firewood and burned the cave, killing all the women inside. He also killed dozens of monks at the Zhou Wang Temple and threw the statues into the toilet. After the rebels were defeated, he escaped and remained unharmed. One day, he suddenly became mentally deranged. His family placed him on a bed, but he soon leaped up and struck himself with his hands, shouting, "The women are coming to demand payment!" He then spoke in a woman's voice, saying, "He has power, we cannot defeat him, we should call the monks." He shouted again, "The monks are here!" From then on, he called out [B17K] day and night without ceasing. After several days, the family members guarding him grew tired and he fled, his whereabouts unknown. Half a month later, someone reported that a man had drowned in the toilet of the Zhou Wang Temple. The family went to investigate and found it was indeed Shen. He was covered in blue-black bruises, with blood flowing from his seven orifices, and the stench was unbearable to approach. Alas! The retribution he received was indeed cruel.

    418

    抚州胡琼，常至城外访其亲串，所过之地荒僻无居人。忽见一女子立水次，亦未之异也。女子顾胡而言曰：“子衣上如何有血污痕？可解衣为子浣之。”胡自视良然，遂解外衣。女子曰：“中衣亦污。”又解之。如是者三，而血痕犹在。回顾女于，嫣然微笑，以手招之。胡悟女子非人也，抱衣却走。女子蹑其后，胡急奔。望见前有巨人二，不知其为人为鬼，亦不遑顾，直造其处，则二巨人者已不见，止有一土地庙。比至庙门，则女子亦不见矣。胡无邪心，故不为女鬼所惑，不然殆哉！

    Explanation

    418

    Tran Quynh, a resident of Fuzhou, often went to the outskirts of the city to visit his relatives and friends. The area he passed through was desolate and uninhabited. Suddenly, he saw a woman standing by the water. He did not find anything unusual at first. The woman looked at him and said, "How did you get a bloodstain on your clothes? Let me wash it for you." Tran Quynh looked at his clothes and realized she was right. He then took off his outer garment. The woman said, "Even the inner layer is stained." He took it off as well. This process was repeated three times, but the bloodstain remained. The woman smiled sweetly and beckoned to him with her hand. Tran Quynh realized that the woman was not human. He picked up his clothes and ran away. The woman followed him. He ran as fast as he could. When he reached a certain place, he saw two giants in front of him, unsure whether they were humans or ghosts, he did not have time to think, and ran straight to them. However, the two giants had already disappeared, leaving only a small temple dedicated to the local land god. By the time he arrived at the temple, the woman had disappeared. Because Tran Quynh had no evil intentions, he was not deceived by the female ghost. Otherwise, it would have been dangerous.

    419

    山阴某甲父子皆嗜食犬，犬之毙其手者不知凡几矣。壬申之秋，其子观剧北城，日晡而归。有卑脚花犬随之行，甚驯扰，试呼之，即弭耳从归。甫入门，忽跳踉大嗥，遽啮其股。其父闻声出，又啮之，伤于足。犬即走出，不知所往。父子中犬毒至深，号呼一昼夜而死。

    　　闵斗阳司马世魁，归安晟舍镇人，官南府通判，升同知。一生正直不苟，居官亦多善政，年老乞归。其卒之前一日，有里人死而复苏，告其家人曰：“阴间路黑不能行，有人语我曰：”明日闵公赴南府城隍任，舆从不少，必有灯火前导，可随之往。沿途有供张，并可得酒食。‘“次日公无疾而终，里人亦死。死后见梦于其母曰：”今日从闵公俱行，不愁昏黑，且幸为公录用，不落寞也。“此事余闻之闵君小圃，即其族也。凡人之聪明正直者，殁则为神，理固可信。先兄壬甫卒于福宁知府任。

    Explanation

    419

    A certain father and son from Shan Yin both loved to eat dog meat; the number of dogs that died by their hands is countless. In the autumn of the year Ren Shen, the son went to watch a play in the northern part of the city and returned at dusk. A short-legged spotted dog followed him, very tame and docile. He tried calling it, and it immediately lowered its ears and followed back. As soon as they entered the door, the dog suddenly jumped and barked loudly, immediately biting the son's thigh. The father heard the noise and came out, and the dog bit him again, injuring his foot. The dog then ran out and disappeared. The father and son were deeply poisoned by the dog and cried out for a day and night before dying.

    Min Douyang, Sīmǎ Shìkuí, was from Gu'an Shengshe Town and served as a Tongpan in Yun Nan Prefecture, later promoted to Tongzhi. He lived a life of integrity and strictness, and his governance was filled with good policies. When he grew old, he requested to return home. The day before he died, a villager died and then revived, telling his family, "The road in the underworld is dark and cannot be traveled, but someone told me: 'Tomorrow Min Gong will take up the position of Chenghuang in Yun Nan Prefecture, with a large entourage, and there will definitely be lights leading the way. You can follow along. There will be provisions along the way, and you can also get food and drink.'" The next day, Min Gong died without any illness, and the villager also died. After death, the villager appeared in a dream to his mother, saying, "Today, I traveled with Min Gong, and we didn't worry about the darkness. I was fortunate to be employed by him and didn't feel lonely." I heard this from Min Jun Xiaopu, who is a member of the same clan. Anyone who is intelligent and upright will become a spirit after death, and this is certainly true. My elder brother Ren Fu died while serving as the Zhi Fu of Fu Ning.

    420

    福宁有杨明经廷瑞者，一乡推长者。先兄既捐馆舍，而杨君亦病，谓家人曰：”吾不起矣。吾梦见青衣者持官符至，言施侯迁擢去，以俞公代之，辟吾为幕僚，期以某日去。吾其能久乎？“趋治后事。果如期而卒。施侯者，故靖海侯晋江施襄壮公，相传殁后为海神者也。于是郡人藉藉，称俞公代施侯为海神云。兄子祖绥曾具先兄事略，求余为传志，其言曰：”明道行状以伊川而传，文忠碑铭待颍滨而作，宏文钜笔，虽亲弗嫌。“余兄弟似皆不足当此语，然吾兄居官立身具有本末，固宜撰次以示后昆。而余衰病侵寻，未能握管，偶因闵公事，附载先兄轶事如此。

    Explanation

    420

    Fu Ning had a man named Yang Tingrui, who was a scholar and highly respected in the village. My elder brother had already passed away, and Yang was also ill. He told his family, "I will not recover. I dreamed that a messenger dressed in official attire came with an official document, saying that Shi Hou had been promoted and replaced by Yu Gong, who appointed me as an advisor and scheduled me to leave on a certain day. How long can I last?" He quickly arranged his after-death affairs. Indeed, he died on the scheduled day. Shi Hou was the late Marquis of Pacifying the Sea from Jinjiang, Shi Xiangzhuang, and it is said that after his death, he became a sea god. Thus, the people of the prefecture talked about it, saying that Yu Gong had replaced Shi Hou as the sea god. My nephew Zuso once compiled a brief account of my elder brother's life and asked me to write a biography. He said, "The biography of Ming Dao was written by Yi Chuan, and the epitaph for Wen Zhong was written by Yingbin. Great works by great writers, even if they are close relatives, are not to be avoided." My brothers and I seem not worthy of such praise, but my elder brother had a clear beginning and end in his official career and personal conduct, so it is appropriate to compile and show to future generations. However, old age and illness have weakened me, and I am unable to hold the pen. Occasionally because of the matter concerning Minister Min, I have included some anecdotes of my elder brother.

    421

    闵君希濂，字一瀛，与余甲辰岁同举于乡者也。尝于夏日在书斋疲[1234]假寐，瞢腾中若有人持名刺来请者，从之至一处，宫殿巍峨，兵卫森列。登其阶，见堂上有古衣冠者二人，本朝衣冠者亦二人，皆南面坐。其两旁列坐者十数人，惟末坐虚焉。持刺者引闵登堂三揖，坐者皆为之起，即引之至末坐曰：“此君坐位也。”少顷，有吏持文书，并以笔墨来，分授两旁坐者，而闵亦得一卷。其卷首书“吏部天官增减司闵”八字，卷尾亦如之。吏请于卷首“闵”字下书一“奉”字，卷尾“闵”字下书一“行”字，而中间文字不使展阅。书已，吏持去，坐者皆散。

    Explanation

    421

    Mǐn Jūn Xī Lián, courtesy name Yī Yíng, was a scholar who passed the provincial examination in the same year as I did, during the Gengchen cycle of the year 1804. One summer day, he was resting in his study and took a brief nap. In a half-awake, half-dream state, he felt as if someone was holding a visiting card and inviting him to come along. Following the person, he arrived at a place where a grand palace stood, surrounded by numerous guards. Upon ascending the steps, he saw two people in ancient attire and two in contemporary attire, all seated facing south on the dais. On either side of them, several others were seated, with only the last seat remaining empty. The person holding the card led Mǐn up to the dais, where he performed three bows, and all the seated people stood up. The person then led him to the empty seat and said, "This is your seat." Shortly after, an official arrived with documents and writing materials, distributing them to the people on either side, and Mǐn also received a scroll. The scroll read "Mǐn of the Zēng Jiǎn Sī of the Lì Bù Tiān Guān" at the top and the same at the bottom. The official asked Mǐn to write the character "fèng" (follow) below his name at the top and "xíng" (act) below his name at the bottom, but the middle text was not to be read. After writing, the official took the scroll away, and all the seated people dispersed.

    422

    前持刺者又引闵至一处，有屋三楹，额曰增减司，告闵曰：“此君之署也。”送之出门而醒。自是频梦至其地，事毕即醒。闵从不与人言，惟所亲者得闻之。暨咸丰之末，江南大乱，镇江府城陷于贼。闵时寓沪上，一日，梦有伟丈夫来见，与之谋克复镇江。闵曰：“书生不谙军旅，何能为？”其人曰：“行军贵谋不贵勇，君其无辞。”旋有诸将戎装而来，请命从何门入。闵梦中率尔曰：“从东门入。”皆曰：“诺。”俄而身履戎行，搴旗斩将，大捷而还，则身固卧逆旅中也。觉劳顿殊甚，静卧三四日乃起，果闻官军收复镇江矣。自是不复梦至增减司治事。闵后为石门教谕，寿终于官，亦无他异。

    Explanation

    422

    A messenger holding a card led Min to a place, where there was a house with three rooms, the sign on the door reading "Department of Increase and Decrease." The messenger told Min, "This is your office." He then escorted Min out the door, and Min woke up. From then on, he frequently dreamed of going to that place, and woke up when the matter was done. Min never told anyone about these dreams, only those close to him could hear about them. By the end of the Xianfeng era, the south was in chaos, and the city of Zhenjiang fell to the rebels. At the time, Min was staying in Shanghai. One day, he dreamed of a great man coming to see him, and they planned to recapture Zhenjiang. Min said, "A scholar is not familiar with military affairs, how can he do anything?" The man replied, "In military campaigns, strategy is valued more than bravery. You should not refuse." Soon, several generals came in military attire, asking for orders on which gate to enter. In his dream, Min quickly said, "Enter through the east gate." They all said, "Yes." Soon, he found himself in military action, taking down flags and cutting down generals, returning with a great victory, but he was actually lying in a guesthouse. He felt extremely tired and stayed quietly in bed for three or four days before getting up. Indeed, he heard that the government troops had recaptured Zhenjiang. From then on, he no longer dreamed of going to the Department of Increase and Decrease to handle affairs. Min later became the magistrate of Shimen, died in office, and there were no other unusual events.

    423

    吴沙阳，归安诸生也。尝客山东，其子年十九矣，在家读书。吴一夕忽梦其子乘马来，遇诸涂，责其子何不下马，不应。追及之，欲与语，其子回顾，举鞭欲鞭之，旁有一白须老人呵止之曰：“此人于汝有十九年养育之恩，不可！不可！”吴遂寤。月余得家书，则其子即于是日死矣。

    Explanation

    423

    Wu Sha Yang, a scholar from Gu'an, once traveled to Shandong. His son, who was nineteen years old, was studying at home. One evening, Wu suddenly dreamed that his son arrived on horseback, met him on the road, and rebuked him for not getting off the horse. His son did not respond. Wu chased him down, wanting to speak to him. His son looked back, lifted his whip as if to strike him. A nearby old man with a white beard shouted and stopped him, saying, "This person has bestowed upon you nineteen years of nurturing grace. You cannot do this! You cannot do this!" Wu then woke up. A month later, he received a letter from home, and it turned out that his son died on that very day.

    424

    罗某业屠，一日将杀豕，豕作人言曰：“我应于明日死，何早也？”乃不杀。次日，又将杀之，豕又言曰：“我应重至九十斤而死，今止八十七斤，何早也？”罗惧，售其豕于人，改业不复屠。柯某亦业屠，一日执豕于牢，闻两豕相对作人语。其一曰：“今日尔去投生矣，我明日亦不免一刀之苦，当随尔去，宜稍待我。”其一曰：“诺”。柯闻之，亦遂改业焉。又有郑某，亦业屠。其徒郑三卧于楼上，夜半忽下楼，自将左手置砧上，以右手举刀断之，血流满身，大呼倒地。咸惊起，俟其苏而问之，言：“见有人来买豚蹄，我割而予之，不知自断其手也。”言毕而死。此三事皆闵君小圃言之。三屠皆湖州乡间人也。

    Explanation

    424

    A butcher named Luo was once about to slaughter a pig, when the pig spoke in human words: "I should die tomorrow, why so soon?" Luo then refrained from killing it. The next day, he was about to slaughter the pig again, and the pig spoke again: "I should weigh ninety catties when I die, but now I only weigh eighty-seven catties, why so soon?" Frightened, Luo sold the pig to someone else and changed his profession, no longer engaging in butchery. Another butcher named Ke also had a pig in his pen one day, and he heard two pigs talking in human language. One said, "Today you are going to be reborn, I will also not escape the pain of a knife tomorrow, I will follow you, so please wait for me a little." The other replied, "Yes." Ke, upon hearing this, also changed his profession. There was also a butcher named Zheng. His apprentice Zheng San was lying on the upper floor, and at midnight, he suddenly came downstairs, placed his left hand on the chopping block himself, and with his right hand, he raised the knife and cut it off. Blood flowed all over his body, and he shouted loudly, falling to the ground. Everyone was shocked, and they waited until he regained consciousness and asked him what happened. Zheng San said, "I saw someone coming to buy pig trotters, I cut it and gave it to them, I didn't know I had cut off my own hand." After saying this, he died. These three events were all told by Mr. Min Xiao Pu. The three butchers were all from the countryside of Huzhou.

    425

    金华府城外有某氏妇者，嫠也。同治壬申年正月望日，村中演戏，尽室往观，妇独不去。至二更后，其夫弟先归，呼妇不应。从窗隙窥之，见室中昏黑，惟妇床前有绿焰一线，如将烬之灯。惧其不戒于火，破扉入，以烛烛之，则见妇独坐椅上，自膝以上皆焦黑如炭，惟口辅不焦，其臭不可向迩。大骇，莫知火所从起。足下一火炉已冷矣，且鞋袜不，则非由炉中起也。此与第五卷所载咸宁毛氏婢事同不可解。

    Explanation

    425

    In the suburbs of Jinhua, there was a certain widow from a family. In the 15th day of the first lunar month in the year 1862 (the year of the Water Rat in the sexagenary cycle), the village was staging a performance, and the whole family went to watch, except for the widow. After the second watch of the night, her husband's younger brother returned home and called out to her, but she did not respond. Looking through the window crack, he saw the room in darkness, with only a green flame, like a nearly extinguished lamp, in front of her bed. Fearing that she might have been careless with fire, he broke down the door to enter and used a candle to illuminate the room. He then saw her sitting alone on a chair, with her legs and above burned black like charcoal, but her mouth and cheeks were not burned, and the smell was unbearable. He was greatly startled and could not understand how the fire had started. The fire stove under her feet was already cold, and her shoes and socks were not burned, so it was not from the stove. This is similar to the case of the servant girl from Xian Ning in the fifth volume, which is also inexplicable.

    426

    仁和典史福建林公汝霖，字小岩，于庚申岁杭城初陷时，衣冠坐堂皇，骂贼，并其家属同死。贼平后，当事者为闻于朝，裒其遣骸，葬于孤山，且立祠焉，今所谓林公祠是也。公有二子，曰庆生，曰泾生，佥谓同死于贼矣。至光绪己卯岁，有楚人罗君来游于浙，遍览湖山之胜。至林公祠，读壁间所刻碑记，知其事实，肃然起敬。留数月，仍还楚。雇一小舟，舟人甚谨愿，问其姓名，曰：“姓李，名泾生，湘乡人也。”问家有何人，曰：“有老父。”问母死几年矣，曰：“小人无母。

    Explanation

    426

    During the reign of Emperor Guangxu, a man from Chu named Luo Jun came to Zhejiang to tour the beautiful landscapes. Upon visiting the Lin Gong Temple, he read the inscriptions on the walls and learned about Lin Rulin's deeds, feeling a deep sense of respect. He stayed for several months before returning to Chu. He hired a small boat, and the boatman was very cautious and diligent. When asked his name, he replied, "My surname is Li, and my given name is Jingsheng. I am from Xiangxiang." When asked about his family, he said, "I have an elderly father." When asked how long it had been since his mother died, he replied, "I have no mother."

    427

    ”异而询其详，曰：“我本福建林氏子，祖官泾县，我生于泾，故名泾生。年八岁，随父官浙，城陷，为贼掠至金陵。有李翁者怜而抚我，即今老父也。后随之亡归，因姓其姓，以操舟为业。李翁无妻，故我无母也。”罗叹曰：“然则尔为林公子矣。”罗有季父官浙中，即驰书告之。其季父言于唐艺农观察，观察言于谭文卿中丞，中丞即移咨湖南，由湘乡令资送来浙。于光绪六年四月十二日至杭州，成服而哭于墓。中丞即以其事入告，俾承袭云骑尉世职焉。林公忠臣，固宜有后，若罗君者亦可谓有心人矣。

    Explanation

    427

    Finding it unusual, Luo asked for details. The boy replied, "I am originally the son of a Lin family from Fujian. My ancestor served as an official in Jing County, and I was born there, hence my name is Jing Sheng. At the age of eight, I followed my father to serve in Zhejiang. When the city fell, I was captured by bandits and taken to Jinling. An elderly man named Li pitied and took care of me, who is now my adoptive father. Later, I fled back home with him and adopted his surname, making a living by operating boats. Li had no wife, so I had no mother." Luo sighed and said, "Then you are the son of Mr. Lin." Luo rode to send a letter to inform his younger uncle, who reported to Governor Tang Yinan. Governor Tang reported to Minister Tan Wenqing, who sent a request to Hunan, and the magistrate of Xiangxiang County funded the boy's return to Zhejiang. On April 12, 1880, the boy arrived in Hangzhou, wore mourning clothes, and cried at the grave. Minister Tan reported the matter, and the boy inherited the hereditary title of Yuniqiwei. Mr. Lin, a loyal subject, certainly deserved to have descendants, and Luo can indeed be called a person of great concern.

    428

    杭州孙氏女，年十五嫁同城高在端。甫五月而粤贼陷城，夫妇偕遁，中途遇贼，妇逸而在端陷焉。贼平后，侦知在端已死，妇赁屋独居，抱他人子为子。时其母亦孑然无依，乃迎之来，同居一室。妇上事其母，下抚其子，惟倚针黹为活，篝火夜作，达旦不休。而性倜傥，遇人则谈笑自如，诙谐间作。母疑其志可夺，阴遣其中表某甲为媒，劝改嫁。妇不可，因此浸失母意，恒以细事谯诃之。妇自夫死，悲伤成疾，又日夜操作，兼为其母所嬲，如是十余年，病转剧。念子已长大，可自立，己病如此，生亦何味，乃乘夜投缳焉。是时其母已寝。忽若有大声呼之起者。急与其子及同居之徐叟偕入妇室，则妇已悬于梁，有一衣茧纟由袍之老者以手藉其足。三人者入，老者始不见。乃解其悬，幸不死。自言初缢时，见有老者力止之，曰：“汝子不恶，汝再苦守十年，必有佳境，神明不负汝苦节也。”此老者疑为社公矣。

    Explanation

    428

    A Woman from the Sun Family in Hangzhou

    A woman from the Sun family in Hangzhou, aged fifteen, married a man named Gao Zaiduan from the same city. After just five months, the Taiping rebels captured the city, and the couple fled together. On the way, they encountered the bandits; the woman escaped, but in the end, the man was captured. After the rebels were defeated, it was learned that the man had died. The woman rented a house and lived alone, adopting another person's child as her own. At that time, her mother was also alone and had no support, so she invited her to come and live together in one room. The woman served her mother above and took care of her son below, relying solely on needlework for a living, working by firelight at night until dawn without stopping. She had a free-spirited and unconstrained nature, talking and laughing naturally when meeting people, occasionally making witty remarks. The mother suspected that her will could be changed, secretly sending a cousin (or relative) named Mr. A to act as a matchmaker, trying to persuade her to remarry. The woman refused, and as a result, gradually lost her mother's favor, always being scolded over trivial matters. The woman became ill from sorrow after her husband's death, and then continued to work day and night, adding to her suffering from her mother's scolding, like this for over ten years, and the illness worsened. Thinking that her son had grown up and could support himself, and that she was so ill, she decided to hang herself at night. At that time, her mother was already asleep. Suddenly, there was a loud voice calling her to wake up. She quickly woke up and went with her son and the co-resident Mr. Xu to enter the woman's room, where they found the woman hanging from the beam, with an old man dressed in a silk robe supporting her feet. When the three people entered, the old man disappeared. They then untied the rope, and fortunately, she did not die. She said that when she first hung herself, she saw an old man who tried to stop her, saying, "Your son is not bad, if you continue to endure for another ten years, you will surely have a good situation, the gods will not fail to reward your steadfast virtue." This old man was likely the village god, the She Gong.

    429

    江浙间凡学手艺者，必三年而成；成后役于其师者三年，不取直。故俗语谓之学三年，帮三年。六年之后，任其所往。若师欲留之，则必予值矣。杭州艮山门外有棕绷店，其徒陆阿毛者六年满矣。师语之曰：“吾有女年十五，与汝年相若也。汝再帮吾三年，吾以女妻汝。”陆欣然从之。又三年，益勤，而其师因家计稍丰，拟嫁其女于贾人子，爽前约。陆请践言，不许。请予值，计三年之值须钱数万，师又吝不与。陆屡以为言，师怒，殴辱之。陆愤甚，缢而死。陆本无家，故无与师为难者。邻比之人始虽不平，师许厚葬之，亦无他言。而每夜闻鬼哭声，达旦始休。又陆之死也，口鼻流血于地，其地至夜辄生碧焰，荧荧如火，高尺许，用水洗涤，竟不能去。师令其女祭之，且祝曰：“嫁后若生子，必以为君后。”自此遂安静无他。

    Explanation

    429

    In the Jiangsu and Zhejiang regions, anyone learning a craft must complete a three-year apprenticeship; after completing the apprenticeship, they serve their master for another three years without receiving payment. Thus, the common saying is "learn for three years, then serve for three years." After six years, the apprentice is free to go where they wish. If the master wishes to keep them, they must be paid. Outside the eastern gate of Hangzhou, there was a palm fiber store, and its apprentice, Lu Amao, had completed his six-year term. The master told him, "I have a daughter who is fifteen years old, and she is about your age. If you help me for another three years, I will marry my daughter to you." Lu gladly agreed. After another three years, he became even more diligent, but his master, due to a slight improvement in their financial situation, planned to marry his daughter to the son of a merchant, breaking the previous agreement. Lu requested to fulfill the promise, but the master refused. He then asked for payment, calculating that three years' worth of payment would amount to several ten thousand coins, but the master was stingy and did not give it. Lu repeatedly brought up the matter, and the master became angry and insulted him. Lu became very angry and hanged himself. Since Lu originally had no family, no one was willing to make trouble for the master. The neighbors at first were not pleased, but the master promised to bury him with great ceremony, and they said nothing more. However, every night they heard ghostly cries until dawn. At the spot where Lu died, blood flowed from his mouth and nose onto the ground, and at night, green flames would emit from the spot, flickering like fire, about a foot high, and even when washed, they could not be removed. The master ordered his daughter to make an offering, and said, "If you give birth to a child, it will be his heir." From then on, everything became peaceful, and there were no more disturbances.

    430

    光绪丙子夏，义乌乡间有某氏儿甫十余龄，夜宿楼上。其父自外纳凉归，上楼欲卧，不见其子，呼之不应。家人闻声毕集，邻比亦至，秉炬穷搜，杳无踪迹。方惊疑问，忽见其子痴立阶前，问所之，不答，良久乃言曰：“有一白发翁到楼上，与儿草屦一双著之，招儿同去曰：”随我去，与汝果子吃。“儿曰：”吾家枣实方熟，食之不尽，岂贪汝果子哉！“翁促之急，儿啼不肯去。翁以手扪儿口，负儿从窗牖出，腾空而去。行一里许，儿闻家人寻呼声，曰：”速放我归，吾家人来矣！“翁乃脱所著草屦，仍负儿归，自墙外遥掷之，若投物然。”竟不知翁为何怪也。

    Explanation

    430

    In the summer of the Bingzi year during the Guangxu era, in a village in Yiwu, there was a boy just in his early teens. He was staying on the upper floor of his house at night. His father returned after cooling off and went upstairs to lie down, but he did not see his son. Calling out to him, he received no response. The family members heard the commotion and gathered, and even neighbors came running. They lit torches and searched thoroughly, but found no trace of the boy. Just as they were beginning to wonder and question, they suddenly saw the boy standing motionless in front of the stairs. When asked where he had been, he did not answer, but after a long pause, he spoke: "An old man with white hair came up to the upper floor, gave me a pair of straw sandals to wear, and invited me to go with him, saying, 'Come with me, and I'll give you some fruit to eat.' I said, 'Our family's dates are just ripe, and there are so many that we can't eat them all—how could I covet your fruit?' The old man urged me to go quickly, but I cried and refused. He then covered my mouth with his hand, carried me out through the window, and soared through the air and went away. We walked about one li, and I heard the sound of my family searching and calling out. I said, 'Quickly release me and let me return, for my family has come!' The old man then removed the straw sandals he was wearing, still carried me back, and threw them far from outside the wall, as if throwing an object." Still, the family did not know what kind of creature the old man was.

    431

    咸丰间，金华之泽塘有操舟者名兴，谈者失其姓。尝在新安江中，有五六人赁其舟，比登岸，出一铜椎准赁舟之值。视其椎，刻龙九条，雕镂精工。其人曰：“此九龙椎也。吾祖为某省军门时所得，珍藏数世矣。”兴受而藏之。及还家，舍舟陆行，路遇一妇，问兴何往，曰：“将之泽塘。”妇请偕行。至一小桥上，妇袖中出酒一壶，粽二枚，与兴曰：“君且食之，妾去即来。”兴食饮已而妇不至，乃携壶前行。有少妇缢于林间，急解救之，问所苦，则其家因失酒、粽，疑妇窃食，妇忿而求死也。兴以壶示其家，且述所遇，始悟为缢鬼所弄也。兴又前行半里许，遇所遇妇人，怒而言曰：“汝坏我事，今不汝贷！”即见怪状吐舌长尺许，兴解囊出椎击之，有光如电，鬼即不见。乃知此椎固神物也。后舟行至七里泷，值大风，舟几覆。兴意椎既灵异，必能御风，以绳系椎投水中，火光迸出，绳绝椎沉，不复可出，而风波果定。

    Explanation

    431

    During the Xianfeng era, there was a boatman named Xing in the wetlands of Jinhua, whose surname is unknown. Once, while he was on the Xin'an River, five or six people hired his boat. After they disembarked, they paid him with a copper mallet as compensation for the boat rental. Upon seeing the mallet, he noticed that it had nine carved dragons, exquisitely detailed. The person holding the mallet said, "This is the Nine-Dragon Mallet. My ancestor acquired it when he was a military governor of a certain province, and it has been treasured for several generations." Xing accepted and kept the mallet. When he returned home, he left the boat and continued on foot. On the road, he met a woman who asked him where he was going. He replied, "I am heading to the wetlands." The woman asked to travel with him. They reached a small bridge, and she took out a flask of wine and two zongzi from her sleeve, saying to Xing, "Please eat these; I will be right back." After Xing had eaten and drunk, the woman did not return. He then carried the flask forward. There was a young woman who had hanged herself in the woods. Xing quickly rescued her and asked what was wrong. She explained that her family had lost the wine and zongzi, suspected her of stealing them, and she was so angry that she sought death. Xing showed the flask to her family and recounted what he had encountered, and they finally realized that they had been deceived by the ghost of the hanged woman. Xing continued forward another half a li and met the woman he had encountered earlier. She angrily said, "You ruined my plan; now I will not forgive you!" She immediately showed a strange appearance with her tongue extended about a foot. Xing opened his bag and took out the mallet to strike her. There was a light like lightning, and the ghost disappeared. He then realized that this mallet was indeed a divine object. Later, when he was traveling by boat to Qililiang, he encountered a strong wind, and the boat nearly capsized. Thinking that the mallet was already magical and mysterious, he believed it must be able to control the wind. He tied the mallet to a rope and threw it into the water. Flames burst out, the rope broke, and the mallet sank, no longer retrievable, but the wind and waves indeed calmed down.

    432

    义乌人陈坡，字东屏，官宁远令。时亻民为乱，前任杨林弃城走，陈至，始平之。先是民间毁一文昌阁，发地得石，有文曰：“龙尾蛇首，犬逐人走，三木齐折，一土独守。”至是乃验三木谓杨林，一土谓坡也。又陈在官时梦游一处，额曰蒲团寺，壁间有诗曰：“破壁颓垣迹已荒，人间几度感沧桑。不如小小蒲团寺，万古钟声到夕阳。”后陈还里，咸丰辛酉贼陷义乌，陈避至城外观音堂，一见愕然曰：“此即梦中所历之蒲团寺也。”明日贼至，遇害。

    　　湖州人朱新甫，于咸丰庚申岁避兵吴江县之莘塔镇。偶坐茶肆，见有一人扶老携幼，叩头于五十余岁一老翁前，且哭且言，情状甚迫，而老翁夷然不顾。朱怪而问之，曰：“吾与此翁，皆盐城人也。吾去岁挈眷属，操小舟，来此卖奄鱼，为贼所劫，一家七口，幸而不死，乞食为活。今遇此翁适将北反，求附其舟，此翁不许。行将作他乡饿莩，是以悲耳。

    Explanation

    432

    Chen Po, a native of Yiwu, courtesy name Dongping, served as the magistrate of Ningyuan. At that time, the common people rose in rebellion, and the previous official, Yang Lin, abandoned the city and fled. When Chen Po arrived, he brought peace to the region. Prior to this, a local had destroyed a Wen Chang Pavilion, and while digging the ground, they unearthed a stone with inscriptions that read: "Dragon tail, snake head, dog chasing man running, three woods all broken, one earth alone guarding." At this point, it was proven that the three woods referred to Yang Lin, and the one earth referred to Chen Po. Chen Po also dreamt of wandering through a place during his tenure as an official, where the entrance was marked as Puzhuan Temple. On the walls, there was a poem that read: "Ruined walls and collapsed ruins, traces already desolate; how many times has the world changed through the ages. Not as good as the small Puzhuan Temple, the sound of the bell reaches the sunset." Later, Chen Po returned to his hometown. In the Xianfeng Xinyou year, bandits captured Yiwu, and Chen Po fled to the Guanyin Hall outside the city. Upon seeing it, he was startled and said, "This is the Puzhuan Temple I dreamt of." The next day, when the bandits arrived, he was killed.

    Zhu Xinfu, a native of Huzhou, fled to Xintazhen in Wujiang County during the Xianfeng Gengshen year to escape the war. One day, while sitting in a teahouse, he saw a man supporting the elderly and carrying the young, bowing deeply in front of a fifty-some-year-old old man, crying and speaking, the situation very urgent, but the old man remained calm and indifferent.
